JUNO [eager]: So I want to start with the Schorske thesis, because it just reframes everything.
CASPAR [dry]: Right, and that's exactly where Carl gets it wrong about the Ringstrasse.
JUNO [excited]: Which is why the whole Secession reading falls apart the moment you bring in the Klimt problem.
CASPAR [knowing]: Exactly. And like we were saying earlier, the liberal bourgeoisie never recovered from it.
JUNO [warm]: Right, which is the thing — he does the same move with them that he does with the other one.
CASPAR [amused]: The other one being the obvious case. Everyone agrees on that part.
JUNO [thoughtful]: Do they though? Because she'd push back hard on that, and honestly she has a point.
CASPAR [conceding]: She would. But that's a different feud, and we don't have time to relitigate it now.
JUNO [knowing]: Anyway — it all comes back to what happened after, which changed how all of this gets read.
CASPAR [wry]: Completely. Once that landed, the whole earlier argument basically inverts on itself.
JUNO [landing it]: So that's really why it still matters. It's the same unresolved thing, just wearing a new coat.
CASPAR [final]: Unresolved is generous. But yes — it's the argument that never actually ended.
